prompt:
I maintain the solitaire corner of our office arcade box and FreeCell is next on the list. I don't need a UI or a solver — I need the ENGINE: load a deal from text, referee every move, and be pedantic about the supermove capacity rule, because that's the one everyone argues about. Implement src/lib.rs in this crate (rust-freecell). tests/freecell.rs and the deals in tests/fixtures/ are the reviewed contract — hands off those, Cargo.toml and .gitignore. Stdlib only.

Cards are two chars, rank A23456789TJQK + suit CDHS (hearts/diamonds red, clubs/spades black). A deal file has 8 lines = cascades 1-8 (blank lines skipped, trailing spaces fine), space-separated cards, first card is the BOTTOM of the cascade, last is the top; cascades 1-4 hold 7 cards, 5-8 hold 6. Game::load(deal: &str) -> Result<Game, String> validates hard, exact messages: "expected 8 cascades, got 7", "cascade 5 must have 6 cards, got 7", "bad card: 9X" (first bad token), "duplicate card: 5H". Sizes + no duplicates = all 52 accounted for.

Moves are 2-char strings, source then destination: cascades '1'-'8', free cells 'a'-'d' (named, so "1c" targets cell c specifically), foundations 'h'. Legal shapes: cascade->cascade, cascade->free, cascade->home, free->cascade, free->home. Anything else — wrong length, unknown chars, same source and dest, free->free — is "bad move: <mv>" verbatim.

apply(mv: &str) -> Result<(), String>; failures never change state. Rules: a free cell holds one card ("free cell a is occupied" / "free cell b is empty"); foundations build A,2,...,K per suit; cascade builds go DOWN one rank in alternating colors; misfits are "does not fit"; taking from an empty cascade is "empty source".

Supermove (cascade->cascade): the top of the source forms a run (maximal descending alternating-color suffix). Onto a non-empty cascade, the number of cards moved k is fixed by whichever run card stacks on the destination top (no such card: "does not fit"); onto an empty cascade, move the LONGEST prefix-of-run that capacity allows. Capacity law, also exposed as max_supermove(&self, to_empty: bool) -> usize: (empty free cells + 1) * 2^(number of empty cascades), except a move INTO an empty cascade must not count that cascade in the multiplier. Over capacity is refused as "supermove needs 6 cards, capacity is 5" (real numbers).

Also: apply_script(&mut self, moves: &str) -> Result<usize, String> runs whitespace-separated moves, returns how many ran, and wraps the first failure as "move 2 (5a): free cell a is occupied" (1-based). is_won() — all four foundations at K. render(), exactly: line 'free: . 8C 7D .' (cells a-d, '.' when empty), line 'home: C:2 D:. H:A S:.' (top rank char per suit in CDHS order), then one line per cascade '1: KC QC JC TC 9C' (bottom to top; an empty cascade is just '1:'), all joined with \n, no trailing newline.

The tests replay a 53-move winning script over the solvable fixture deal (is_won must flip exactly at the end), drive a supermove-heavy sequence over the second deal pinning capacities and full renders at each stage, and poke every error path with exact messages. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
